findings.description: The North Carolina state seal/logo image present at the top of the PDF is missing in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has the official NC state seal image centered at the top. The HTML version has no image, only the text 'North Carolina Department of Revenue'.

findings.description: The header layout with Governor name on left and Secretary name on right is not properly preserved with the same visual spacing/alignment.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Pat McCory Governor' left-aligned and 'Lyons Gray Secretary' right-aligned with clear spatial separation. HTML preserves the names but the layout alignment differs somewhat, though both names are present.

findings.description: The 'Re: Private Letter Ruling' line loses its indentation formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Re: Private Letter Ruling' indented under the Attention line. HTML shows it left-aligned without indentation.

findings.description: The Account ID and Federal ID lines are right-aligned in the PDF but appear slightly differently positioned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF has these fields clearly right-justified. HTML maintains them on the right side but with slightly different positioning.

findings.description: The indentation of numbered questions and responses differs between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows questions 1 and 2 with specific indentation and 'Response:' in bold. HTML preserves the bold formatting but with slightly different indentation structure.

findings.description: The signature block and cc section alignment differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF has 'Very truly yours' and the Administration Officer title centered/right-aligned. HTML has them right-aligned as well but with slightly different spacing.

findings.description: Multiple <main> elements are used in the HTML, creating a broken document structure. The first <main> from the outer wrapper contains a nested <main> element for page 1 content, and another <main> for page 2 content. This is structurally invalid.
findings.evidence: The HTML has three <main> tags: one from the outer template wrapper, one for page 1 content, and one for page 2 content.

findings.description: The page header 'SUPLR 2013-0022' appears three times in the HTML (once in the first page-header div, once in the page-footer area between pages, and inherently in the document). In the PDF it appears as a header on each page, so this is acceptable as header reproduction, but the first instance is inside the outer <main> while the second is outside the nested <main>, creating a slightly redundant structure.
findings.evidence:
Two instances of
SUPLR 2013-0022
in the HTML.

findings.description: The second image has an empty alt text attribute, which is not descriptive. The first image's alt text 'North Carolina state seal' is reasonable.
findings.evidence: Page 2 image:

findings.description: The PDF contains a hyperlink to www.dornc.com, but in the HTML the URL is rendered as plain text rather than as a clickable link, unlike the PDF where it appears as a hyperlinked URL.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Website: www.dornc.com' (hyperlinked). HTML: 'Website: www.dornc.com' (plain text in footer, no <a> tag).
